questions.question: Explain the difference between & and && operators in Java with an example.
keyPoints: & is logical AND that evaluates both operands
&& is short-circuit AND that evaluates second operand only if first is true
Example showing side-effect difference using increment operator

questions.question: What is short-circuit evaluation? When would you prefer to use non-short-circuit operators?
keyPoints: Short-circuit evaluates only necessary operands
&& stops if first is false, || stops if first is true
Use non-short-circuit when second expression has side effects that must execute

questions.question: State and explain De Morgan's Laws with an example each.
keyPoints: First law: !(A && B) = !A || !B
Second law: !(A || B) = !A && !B
Example with actual boolean values demonstrating the equality

questions.question: What is the XOR operator in Java? Write the truth table for XOR.
keyPoints: XOR (^) returns true when exactly one operand is true
Truth table: true^true=false, true^false=true, false^true=true, false^false=false
Used in scenarios requiring exclusive conditions

questions.question: Explain the operator precedence for boolean operators in Java.
keyPoints: ! (NOT) has highest precedence
&& (AND) has next precedence
|| (OR) has lowest precedence
Parentheses can override default precedence

questions.question: Write a Java program to demonstrate the difference between && and & operators using side effects.
keyPoints: Use increment/decrement in second operand
Show && skips evaluation when first is false
Show & always evaluates both operands
Display results to prove the difference

questions.question: Explain all boolean logical operators in Java with their truth tables and write a Java program to verify De Morgan's Laws.
keyPoints: Cover !, &, |, ^, &&, || operators with truth tables
Explain short-circuit vs non-short-circuit
Write program that tests both De Morgan's laws
Show actual boolean values verify the laws

questions.question: Write a Java program that uses boolean logical operators to determine if a year is a leap year. A year is a leap year if divisible by 4 but not by 100, or divisible by 400.
keyPoints: Use && and || operators in the condition
Test with leap years (2000, 2024) and non-leap years (1900, 2023)
Explain how boolean operators control program flow

questions.question: Discuss the importance of boolean logical operators in decision-making in Java. Create a program that uses nested boolean expressions to validate user input.
keyPoints: Boolean operators enable conditional execution
Used in if-else, while loops, for conditions
Program validates multiple conditions like range, format, required fields
Show complex boolean expression building

questions.question: Compare and contrast logical AND (&) with short-circuit AND (&&), and logical OR (|) with short-circuit OR (||). When would you use each?
keyPoints: & and | always evaluate both operands
&& and || use short-circuit evaluation
Use &&/|| for efficiency in normal conditions
Use &/| when side effects in second operand must execute
Example demonstrating both scenarios
